name: World Finance
description_short: World Finance Forest Park, Georgia — World Finance offers in-person personal installment loans, credit building, tax prep, and roadside assistance throu...
description_long:
World Finance (Loans by World) is a national personal finance company operating brick-and-mortar branches in local communities across the United States. The Memphis branch at 4593 Elvis Presley Boulevard serves the 38116 ZIP code and is led by Branch Manager Rutherford Brannon. The company positions itself as a community-rooted lender — staff members live and work in the neighborhoods they serve, emphasizing personal relationships over automated online processes.

The branch offers personal installment loans as its core product, alongside credit building programs, tax preparation services, refund advance loans, and a motor club roadside assistance plan. This bundled approach allows customers to handle multiple financial needs in a single location. The branch also offers one-on-one consultations to walk borrowers through loan terms, qualifying documents, and repayment structures before they sign anything.

What distinguishes World Finance from online-only lenders is its in-person, relationship-driven model. Staff are trained to work with customers across varying financial situations, including those with thin or damaged credit histories. The branch maintains bilingual service (Hablamos Español), making it accessible to Spanish-speaking borrowers. Google reviews specifically highlight staff professionalism, responsiveness, and willingness to walk customers through their contracts in detail — an uncommon practice in consumer lending.

World Finance is best suited for borrowers who want face-to-face service, have limited or no credit history, or are actively trying to build credit through a structured installment loan. However, the branch model means limited hours (Monday–Friday 9AM–5:30PM, Saturday by phone only, closed Sunday), and the website discloses no APR ranges, loan amounts, or fee structures — prospective borrowers must visit or call to get specific terms. Loan costs for subprime installment lenders in this segment can be significant, so comparing offers before committing is advisable.

As a financial institution, this lender competes with both traditional banks and newer fintech personal loan lenders in the consumer lending space. Borrowers seeking personal loans for bad credit may find more flexible terms through online lenders, while those focused on simplifying payments may benefit from debt consolidation loans with fixed rates. For credit building, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to improvement. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility into credit health, and credit counseling through nonprofit agencies can help consumers create sustainable budgeting plans. Many of these lenders offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments over 12 to 60 months, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
address: 526 Forest Pkwy Suite I, Forest Park, GA 30297

company_info.headquarters: 526 Forest Pkwy Suite I, Forest Park, GA 30297

pros: In-person, one-on-one loan consultations — staff walk borrowers through contracts and terms
Bilingual service available (Hablamos Español) for Spanish-speaking customers
Bundled financial services: loans, tax prep, refund advance, and roadside assistance in one branch
Credit building focus — explicitly serves customers building long-term credit history
Community-embedded staff — branch employees live in the Memphis neighborhood they serve
Refund advance loans available for tax season cash flow needs
Consistent weekday hours (9AM–5:30PM Monday through Friday)
cons: No APR ranges, loan amounts, or fee disclosures on the website — must call or visit to get terms
Branch-only model with no evident fully online application or digital funding option
Closed Sundays; Saturday hours require calling the branch — limited weekend access
Single branch reviewed here — availability depends entirely on having a local World Finance location
best_for: First-time borrowers with no credit history seeking a structured installment loan
Spanish-speaking consumers who want bilingual in-person loan service
Borrowers who prefer face-to-face guidance over online-only lending platforms
Customers wanting to combine tax prep and a loan or refund advance in one visit
diagnosis: World Finance is best for consumers with limited or no credit who want hands-on, in-person support from a lender that explicitly works with non-prime borrowers and bundles tax prep and credit building alongside loans. The main caveat is transparency: no rates or loan amounts are published online, so borrowers must engage directly to understand costs — and should compare offers carefully before committing, as installment lenders serving this segment often carry above-average APRs.
